action.hypothesis: The baseline topk K=128 EF=12 signum recipe may be slightly underpowered at LR=8e-4; increasing LR to 1e-3 can test whether the worse 6e-4 result came from under-training rather than instability.
action.summary: Keep the topk mainline recipe fixed and run the opposite single-variable learning-rate probe at LR=1e-3 for K=128 EF=12 with signum.
action.self_review: This stays on the prescribed mainline path: same family, same K, same optimizer, one LR-only question. Since 6e-4 regressed, the most informative next move is to bracket the baseline from the other side before switching optimizers.
notes_to_memory: Round 3 isolates the opposite LR direction to determine whether the topk mainline is limited by under-training at 8e-4 or already near its best signum LR.
next_hypotheses: If LR=1e-3 also fails to beat the baseline, keep LR at 8e-4 and test OPTIMIZER=adam as the next single-variable change.
If LR=1e-3 improves early progress but degrades late stability, try a milder LR increase such as 9e-4 only if another LR round is still justified before optimizer swap.

result.description: Keep the topk mainline recipe fixed and run the opposite single-variable learning-rate probe at LR=1e-3 for K=128 EF=12 with signum.
result.self_review: This stays on the prescribed mainline path: same family, same K, same optimizer, one LR-only question. Since 6e-4 regressed, the most informative next move is to bracket the baseline from the other side before switching optimizers.
